summary: Russian forces used ZALA Z-16 drones to adjust FAB-500 bomb strikes that destroyed a bridge in Pisantsy, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. ZALA Lancet loitering munitions operators are employing tactics to hit multiple enemy targets with single strikes despite EW interference. A Ukrainian drone attack on Novorossiysk Bay struck the Russian frigate "Admiral Essen," detonating PK-10 grenade launchers and damaging the TK-25 EW complex. Ukrainian operators conducted FPV strikes over 50 km into occupied Donetsk region, hitting military and logistics vehicles. Russian air defense units, particularly the Rubicon Center, intercepted over 1,500 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs in a single day, with operators destroying 107 targets on March 6 and 88 on March 7. Ukrainian firm Vyriy is developing new "Lightning" UAV variants with retargeting systems, while Russian forces are reportedly deploying fixed-wing UAVs carrying two FPV drones each.
